commits.subject: add init-release command for baseline tagging
commits.body:
Allows existing projects to adopt bumpcraft from a clean slate:
  bumpcraft init-release --version 1.0.0

Creates a git tag, updates package.json, records in release history.
Future releases only look at commits after the baseline tag.

Options: --force (overwrite tag), --allow-dirty, --message, --no-commit
commits.raw:
c1fba983ed3501c1feed4d351c1dcd2ff4ceb389 feat: add init-release command for baseline tagging
Allows existing projects to adopt bumpcraft from a clean slate:
  bumpcraft init-release --version 1.0.0

Creates a git tag, updates package.json, records in release history.
Future releases only look at commits after the baseline tag.

Options: --force (overwrite tag), --allow-dirty, --message, --no-commit

commits.subject: CI pipeline — skip bot commits, proper error handling, conditional npm publish
commits.body:
- Skip release job when commit is from the bot (prevents infinite loop)
- Validate step properly handles exit code 2 (no release) vs errors
- Uses built dist/cli/index.js instead of npx bumpcraft
- npm publish only runs if NPM_TOKEN secret is configured

commits.subject: enable CI/CD pipeline — tests on push/PR, auto-release on main
commits.body:
- Tests run on Node 20 and 22 for every push and PR
- On main: bumpcraft releases itself (version bump, changelog, GitHub release)
- Commits release artifacts (package.json, CHANGELOG.md, .bumpcraft/)
- Publishes to npm automatically via NPM_TOKEN secret
- Skips release if no conventional commits since last tag

- On main: bumpcraft releases itself (version bump, changelog, GitHub release)
- Commits release artifacts (package.json, CHANGELOG.md, .bumpcraft/)
- Publishes to npm automatically via NPM_TOKEN secret
- Skips release if no conventional commits since last tag

commits.subject: rich changelog with emoji sections, commit links, and compare URLs
commits.body:
- Changelog sections use emoji labels (🚀 Features, 🐛 Bug Fixes, etc.)
- Commit hashes link to GitHub commit pages when GITHUB_REPOSITORY is set
- Version header links to the GitHub release page
- Compare link shows diff between previous and new version
- Breaking changes section with ⚠ at the top
- Sections ordered by importance (features → fixes → perf → refactor → docs)
- Atomic CHANGELOG.md write via tmp+rename
- Tests updated for new format

commits.subject: address seventh-round audit — dashboard build + query param safety
commits.body:
- I1: build script now copies src/dashboard/index.html to dist/ after
  tsc, so the dashboard actually exists in production/Docker deployments
  instead of silently returning 404
- I2: changelog and history API routes use queryString() helper to safely
  extract string-only query params; duplicate/array params are ignored
  instead of being unsafely cast to string
commits.raw:
f1c43f189087bd4155a2a9094303fdb4b88e001b fix: address seventh-round audit — dashboard build + query param safety
- I1: build script now copies src/dashboard/index.html to dist/ after
  tsc, so the dashboard actually exists in production/Docker deployments
  instead of silently returning 404
- I2: changelog and history API routes use queryString() helper to safely
  extract string-only query params; duplicate/array params are ignored
  instead of being unsafely cast to string

commits.subject: address sixth-round audit — last two stragglers
commits.body:
- I1: validate command's inline action handler now wrapped in try/catch
  matching all other CLI commands (was the only one missed in round 5)
- I2: docker-compose.yml passes GITHUB_TOKEN and GITHUB_REPOSITORY into
  the container environment so the GitHub release plugin actually fires
commits.raw:
e8e6cf93e47bca662f088984deb75539f523e2a9 fix: address sixth-round audit — last two stragglers
- I1: validate command's inline action handler now wrapped in try/catch
  matching all other CLI commands (was the only one missed in round 5)
- I2: docker-compose.yml passes GITHUB_TOKEN and GITHUB_REPOSITORY into
  the container environment so the GitHub release plugin actually fires

commits.subject: address fifth-round audit — final hardening
commits.body:
- I1: github plugin validates repo format against owner/name pattern
  to prevent SSRF via path traversal in the GitHub API URL
- I2: all 7 CLI command action handlers wrapped in try/catch with
  clean error messages and process.exit(1) instead of stack traces
- I3: --force-bump validates major/minor/patch in CLI; runRelease
  normalizes forceBump early so invalid values like 'none' are
  treated as undefined instead of crashing with internal error
- M1: Dockerfile adds non-root user (bumpcraft) via USER directive
- M2: Dockerfile uses --omit=dev instead of deprecated --only=production
- M3: Dockerfile adds HEALTHCHECK for orchestrator liveness detection
commits.raw:
8a39921cfdd1e5c6b23e1757b48313472609ff58 fix: address fifth-round audit — final hardening
- I1: github plugin validates repo format against owner/name pattern
  to prevent SSRF via path traversal in the GitHub API URL
- I2: all 7 CLI command action handlers wrapped in try/catch with
  clean error messages and process.exit(1) instead of stack traces
- I3: --force-bump validates major/minor/patch in CLI; runRelease
  normalizes forceBump early so invalid values like 'none' are
  treated as undefined instead of crashing with internal error
- M1: Dockerfile adds non-root user (bumpcraft) via USER directive
- M2: Dockerfile uses --omit=dev instead of deprecated --only=production
- M3: Dockerfile adds HEALTHCHECK for orchestrator liveness detection

commits.subject: address fourth-round audit — all remaining issues resolved
commits.body:
CRITICAL:
- C1: HistoryStore.writeLock now uses separate chain so a rejected save
  doesn't poison future saves; error still surfaces to the failing caller
- C2: git log uses --format=%H %s%n%b%x00 with NUL separator so commit
  bodies are fetched; BREAKING CHANGE: in footers now correctly triggers
  major bumps end-to-end (added integration test to prove it)
- C3: HistoryStore.getAll validates JSON.parse result is an array;
  corrupted history files no longer crash all callers with TypeError

IMPORTANT:
- I1: github plugin guards nextVersion null before use; preRelease check
  now uses non-optional access after the guard
- I2: HistoryStore.query returns [] when from/to version not found
  instead of silently returning the full history
- I3: API key comparison uses crypto.timingSafeEqual to prevent
  timing side-channel attacks
- I4: GroupManager validates group names against /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/
  in all public methods to prevent path traversal
- I5: freezeAfter validated by Zod refine — invalid day names now
  throw CONFIG_ERROR instead of silently disabling the freeze
- I6: PackageJsonSource.write is now atomic via tmp + rename

MINOR:
- M2: HistoryStore.query tests added for since, from/to, last, and
  combined filters including not-found cases
- M3: inquirer removed from dependencies (never imported anywhere)
- M4: policy test description corrected (null = unlimited, not 0)
- M5: CLI --last validates positive integer; exits 1 with clear message
- M6: GroupManager.delete and path-traversal rejection now tested
- M7: api.test.ts cleans up BUMPCRAFT_API_KEY in afterEach
- M8: integration test uses vi.resetModules() in beforeEach for
  proper ESM module isolation between tests

- C2: git log uses --format=%H %s%n%b%x00 with NUL separator so commit
  bodies are fetched; BREAKING CHANGE: in footers now correctly triggers
  major bumps end-to-end (added integration test to prove it)
- C3: HistoryStore.getAll validates JSON.parse result is an array;
  corrupted history files no longer crash all callers with TypeError

IMPORTANT:
- I1: github plugin guards nextVersion null before use; preRelease check
  now uses non-optional access after the guard
- I2: HistoryStore.query returns [] when from/to version not found
  instead of silently returning the full history
- I3: API key comparison uses crypto.timingSafeEqual to prevent
  timing side-channel attacks
- I4: GroupManager validates group names against /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/
  in all public methods to prevent path traversal

- I6: PackageJsonSource.write is now atomic via tmp + rename

MINOR:

  combined filters including not-found cases
- M3: inquirer removed from dependencies (never imported anywhere)
- M4: policy test description corrected (null = unlimited, not 0)
- M5: CLI --last validates positive integer; exits 1 with clear message
- M6: GroupManager.delete and path-traversal rejection now tested
- M7: api.test.ts cleans up BUMPCRAFT_API_KEY in afterEach
- M8: integration test uses vi.resetModules() in beforeEach for
  proper ESM module isolation between tests

commits.subject: address third-round audit issues (N1-N9, N11-N14)
commits.body:
- N1: bumpPreRelease guards against NaN counter (non-numeric suffix like
  beta.rc no longer produces beta.NaN)
- N2: HistoryStore.query applies since filter on full list before from/to
  slicing so version lookups are accurate when filters are combined
- N3: validateReleaseBody rejects non-object bodies (string/array/null)
  to prevent validation bypass on malformed JSON payloads
- N4: GroupManager.delete wraps unlink; ENOENT is silently ignored,
  other errors surface with a descriptive message
- N5: interactive.ts defaults to vi on non-Windows; release command
  catches editor failures and continues without edits
- N6: HistoryStore atomic write via tmp file + rename to prevent
  partial-write from permanently wiping history on crash
- N7: changelog CLI exits with code 2 when no changes found, matching
  the validate command and enabling scripted pipeline detection
- N8: GET /api/history validates that last is a positive integer,
  returns 400 for non-numeric or negative values
- N9: changelogJsonPlugin falls back to 'unknown' for nextVersion so
  the version key is never silently dropped from JSON history entries
- N11: SemVer._compare uses > / < (ASCII) instead of localeCompare for
  semver spec §11.4.4 compliance
- N12: GroupManager.list uses slice(0,-5) for safe .json suffix removal
- N13: server.ts error handler gives a user-friendly message on EADDRINUSE
- N14: integration test uses git init -b main to work regardless of
  the user's init.defaultBranch git config
commits.raw:
d662b0b90ef6cf4e87631050c66e3d54b1fc1b3e fix: address third-round audit issues (N1-N9, N11-N14)
- N1: bumpPreRelease guards against NaN counter (non-numeric suffix like
  beta.rc no longer produces beta.NaN)
- N2: HistoryStore.query applies since filter on full list before from/to
  slicing so version lookups are accurate when filters are combined
- N3: validateReleaseBody rejects non-object bodies (string/array/null)
  to prevent validation bypass on malformed JSON payloads
- N4: GroupManager.delete wraps unlink; ENOENT is silently ignored,
  other errors surface with a descriptive message
- N5: interactive.ts defaults to vi on non-Windows; release command
  catches editor failures and continues without edits
- N6: HistoryStore atomic write via tmp file + rename to prevent
  partial-write from permanently wiping history on crash
- N7: changelog CLI exits with code 2 when no changes found, matching
  the validate command and enabling scripted pipeline detection
- N8: GET /api/history validates that last is a positive integer,
  returns 400 for non-numeric or negative values
- N9: changelogJsonPlugin falls back to 'unknown' for nextVersion so
  the version key is never silently dropped from JSON history entries
- N11: SemVer._compare uses > / < (ASCII) instead of localeCompare for
  semver spec §11.4.4 compliance
- N12: GroupManager.list uses slice(0,-5) for safe .json suffix removal
- N13: server.ts error handler gives a user-friendly message on EADDRINUSE
- N14: integration test uses git init -b main to work regardless of
  the user's init.defaultBranch git config

commits.subject: address second-round audit issues (C1-C3, I1-I9 + minor fixes)
commits.body:
- C1: HistoryStore.save serializes writes via a promise chain to prevent
  concurrent read-modify-write corruption
- C2: GroupManager.create re-throws non-ENOENT errors instead of silently
  overwriting group data on permission errors or corrupt files
- C3: config.ts only suppresses ENOENT; SyntaxError in JSON config now
  throws BumpcraftError(CONFIG_ERROR) instead of falling back to defaults
- I1: PipelineRunner uses continue instead of break at changelog stage so
  release/notify stages still run when a resolve plugin sets nextVersion
- I2: CLI release command locks in the policy-checked bumpType for the
  actual release call to prevent race-condition over/under-bumping
- I3: GitHub plugin throws on non-422 API failures (propagates via
  PipelineRunner error isolation) instead of silently returning
- I4: authMiddleware uses 401 for missing key (no env var name leakage),
  403 only for wrong key; generic error messages throughout
- I5: HistoryStore.query returns empty array when since version not found
  instead of returning all entries
- I6: group release preserves group when commits produce no releasable bump
- I7: SemVer._compare correctly handles numeric-vs-alphanumeric pre-release
  identifiers per semver spec §11.4.1
- I8: dashboard HTML cached at app startup (readFileSync once) instead of
  blocking the event loop on every request
- I9: dashboard uses textContent/createElement instead of innerHTML to
  prevent XSS via API response values
- Minor: openInEditor cleans up temp file in finally block
commits.raw:
9cca77ae0d982f7e1a980be82b9ab3a4e4095b5e fix: address second-round audit issues (C1-C3, I1-I9 + minor fixes)
- C1: HistoryStore.save serializes writes via a promise chain to prevent
  concurrent read-modify-write corruption
- C2: GroupManager.create re-throws non-ENOENT errors instead of silently
  overwriting group data on permission errors or corrupt files
- C3: config.ts only suppresses ENOENT; SyntaxError in JSON config now
  throws BumpcraftError(CONFIG_ERROR) instead of falling back to defaults
- I1: PipelineRunner uses continue instead of break at changelog stage so
  release/notify stages still run when a resolve plugin sets nextVersion
- I2: CLI release command locks in the policy-checked bumpType for the
  actual release call to prevent race-condition over/under-bumping
- I3: GitHub plugin throws on non-422 API failures (propagates via
  PipelineRunner error isolation) instead of silently returning
- I4: authMiddleware uses 401 for missing key (no env var name leakage),
  403 only for wrong key; generic error messages throughout
- I5: HistoryStore.query returns empty array when since version not found
  instead of returning all entries
- I6: group release preserves group when commits produce no releasable bump
- I7: SemVer._compare correctly handles numeric-vs-alphanumeric pre-release
  identifiers per semver spec §11.4.1
- I8: dashboard HTML cached at app startup (readFileSync once) instead of
  blocking the event loop on every request
- I9: dashboard uses textContent/createElement instead of innerHTML to
  prevent XSS via API response values
- Minor: openInEditor cleans up temp file in finally block

commits.subject: address important audit issues (I1/I2/I3/I5/I6/I7/I10)
commits.body:
- I1: wrap PackageJsonSource.write() in try/catch with BumpcraftError
- I2: enforce maxBumpPerDay in PolicyEngine.check(bumpType, todayCount)
  and compute today's release count in the CLI before checking
- I3: add requiresConfirmation to PolicyResult based on autoRelease list;
  CLI auto-enables interactive mode when bumpType not in autoRelease
- I5: freezeAfter now blocks 1–2 days after freeze day (covers weekend)
- I6: GitHub plugin handles 422 by fetching the existing release
- I7: getCommitsSince uses git.raw(['log', range, '--format=%H %s'])
  for reliable output instead of simple-git's structured log parser
- I10: validate preRelease/forceBump/from on POST /api/release and
  /api/release/dry-run, returning 400 on invalid input
commits.raw:
da086ab4ad9c5432d7b84550948f9a2fdd8973d9 fix: address important audit issues (I1/I2/I3/I5/I6/I7/I10)
- I1: wrap PackageJsonSource.write() in try/catch with BumpcraftError
- I2: enforce maxBumpPerDay in PolicyEngine.check(bumpType, todayCount)
  and compute today's release count in the CLI before checking
- I3: add requiresConfirmation to PolicyResult based on autoRelease list;
  CLI auto-enables interactive mode when bumpType not in autoRelease
- I5: freezeAfter now blocks 1–2 days after freeze day (covers weekend)
- I6: GitHub plugin handles 422 by fetching the existing release
- I7: getCommitsSince uses git.raw(['log', range, '--format=%H %s'])
  for reliable output instead of simple-git's structured log parser
- I10: validate preRelease/forceBump/from on POST /api/release and
  /api/release/dry-run, returning 400 on invalid input

Adds full-pipeline integration test against a temp git repo, Dockerfile and docker-compose for the API, and fixes Zod v4 / simple-git TypeScript compatibility (z.record now requires 2 args, object .default() requires factory, simpleGit changed to named import).

commits.subject: scaffold bumpcraft project structure
commits.body:
Initialize bumpcraft as an ESM TypeScript project with npm, configure
TypeScript (NodeNext modules), Vitest for testing, and set up all source
and test directory trees for the pluggable semver engine.
commits.raw:

changelogOutput:
## 1.0.1 (2026-03-18)

### Documentation

- add deployment guide, update docker.md and README ([1d9c531])
- add comprehensive documentation ([7cbabc7])

### Bug Fixes

- address seventh-round audit — dashboard build + query param safety ([f1c43f1])
- address sixth-round audit — last two stragglers ([e8e6cf9])
- address fifth-round audit — final hardening ([8a39921])
- address fourth-round audit — all remaining issues resolved BREAKING ([22cc5eb])
- address third-round audit issues (N1-N9, N11-N14) ([d662b0b])
- address second-round audit issues (C1-C3, I1-I9 + minor fixes) ([9cca77a])
- address important audit issues (I1/I2/I3/I5/I6/I7/I10) ([da086ab])
- resolve critical audit issues (SemVer comparison, forceBump, branch strategy) ([ca2b42e])

### Features

- add integration tests and Docker setup ([b19b589])
- add REST API, CLI, library API, and release dashboard ([77fb922])
- add plugins, policy engine, history store, and release groups ([c062f9b])
- add GitClient, VersionSource, and PipelineRunner ([26f8e64])
- add core library (errors, logger, SemVer, config, BumpResolver, pipeline types) ([005634b])
- scaffold bumpcraft project structure ([d904af9])
